def: s.f. Forest leaves; wild fruit; grass, herbage, vegetation, the vegetable kingdom.
more: n. m. H. ban forest, pattā leaf. 1. Forest leaves; wild fruit. 2. The vegetable kingdom.

def: adj. Having, or adorned with, gold or silver edging; — s.f. Lace; band or riband studded with spangles; gold or silver lace or edging worn on a cap or mantle.
more: n. f. Lace; a riband studded with spangles; gold or silver lace worn on the hat.
s. f. Lace, a riband studded with spangles.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic بِنْت (bint).

def: s.f. Broad-cloth.
more: n. f. Broadcloth. bannāt kī hālit , Slang. adj. Red as scarlet; very red.

def: s.f. Broad-cloth = banāt, q.v. s.f. Broad-cloth.

ety: of بنت bint, fem. of ibn or bin, rt. بني
def: s.f. The tender herbage
subs: s.m. The constellation of the Greater or Lesser Bear (consisting of seven stars, four of which are conceived to bear the resemblance of a naʻsh or bier, and the remaining three to be the banāt or daughters preceding it).

def: adj. Made of broad-cloth; woollen.
more: adj. Woollen, made of broad cloth.

def: adj. Woollen; made of broad-cloth.

s. m. The constellation of the Greater or Lesser Bear, consisting of seven stars; four of which are conceived to bear the resemblance of a bier (نعش), and the remaining three to be the (بنات) daughters preceding it.
s.f. Fighting with cudgels; club-fight.
n. f. Fighting with cudgels; club fight.
(from بنانا) s. f. Preparation.
s.f. Getting ready, preparing; preparation; art, skill, dexterity.

s.f. Make, structure, construction, build, formation, composition, manufacture, workmanship, work; frame, form, fashion, figure, shape, mould, conformation, formation, appearance; fabrication, invention, contrivance, art, artifice, affectation; fiction, sham, pretence; forgery, counterfeit; show, display, embellishment, get up.
more: (from بنانا) s. f. Make, invention, affectation, contrivance, fabrication, formation, fiction, sham.
wety: From بَنْنَا (bannā, “to make”) + ـَاوَٹ (-āvaṭ).

def: adj. Ninety-two.
part. (of banānā), Made, &c.
subs: v.n. To be making, be in hand (e.g. banāyā jātā hai, 'It is being made').

def: v. n. To be making, to be in hand.
s.m. Eel.
s.f. Prating, jabber, senseless talk
subs: To prate, talk foolishly, &c.; to blurt out (a secret, &c.); to make a disagreeable noise, bellow, howl.

def: s.f. = baṅwar, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. بن for this and all compounds having ban for the first member.
s. m. Living in the forest, forester.
adj. Inhabiting the forest; forester, inhabitant of the woods.
s. m. Wild ( baiṅgan ) egg-plant. (Solanum melongena).

ety: S. वल्मीक; — cf. S. वाम, and H. bām and bāṅb
def: s.f. Hillock or ground thrown up by white ants, ant-hill; snake's hole (since ant-hills deserted by ants are commonly taken possession of by snakes).

ety: S. वल्मीक; — cf. S. वाम, and H. bām and bāṅb
def: s.f. Hillock or ground thrown up by white ants, ant-hill; snake's hole (since ant-hills deserted by ants are commonly taken possession of by snakes).

ety: S. वल्मीक; — cf. S. वाम, and H. bām and bāṅb
def: s.f. Hillock or ground thrown up by white ants, ant-hill; snake's hole (since ant-hills deserted by ants are commonly taken possession of by snakes).
more: Tir. n. f. S. वाम a snake. A snake's hole; an ant-hole. 1. Ghar āyā nāg na pūjye, aur bāṅbī pūjan jāëṅ . Prov. The snake-god in her house she worshipped not, Then all devoutly she the snake-hole sought. (To run in vain after lost opportunity). 2. Sāṅp sab jagah ṭeṛhā chaltā hai, par apnī bāṅbī meṅ sīdhā jātā hai . Prov. The snake's course is always crooked, but he goes straight to his own hole. (Crooked with others, true to one's own). 3. Bāṅbī wā kī jal bharī, ūpar jārī āg; Jabhī bajāī bāṅsurī, nikso kālo nāg . Riddle. Ans. Huqqā . A snake-hole full of water is, a fire burns on its neck, No sooner on your pipe you play, comes out a serpent black.

def: s. m. A wild cat.
s.f. Reading, elocution (see next).

v.t. = baćānā, q.v. v.t. To save, preserve, rescue, deliver, extricate, set free; to sustain, support, protect; to secure, defend, guard, shield, screen, cloak; to conceal, hide; to set aside, put or lay by, save, spare, leave; to reserve, keep back (a part of anything fraudulently); to avoid, keep clear of, move out of the way
subs: v.n. To keep (oneself) clear of, to avoid, escape, get off scot free
(-ko), To carry off safe, to save, secure
baćāne-hārā, baćāne-hār, baćān-hār, s.m. Deliverer, saviour, preserver, protector.

def: v.t. To cause to read, to make, or hear (one) read ( = baṅćwānā).
more: v. a. (dakh. caus. of بچنا bachnā ) To save, deliver, defend, free, &c.: see بچانا bachānā.
v. a. (caus. of بانچنا bānchnā ) To cause to read.

def: s.f. Wish, desire.
more: n. f. S. वाञ्छ to desire. Desire; wish ( ichchhā ). man-bānchhat phal , n. m. Heart-felt desire; wished-for object; the desired effect.

def: s. m. A monkey.
s. (dakh.) See بچاؤ bachāʼo.
part. adj. Wished for, desired, longed for.

ety: Prk. वांछणअं = S. वाञ्छनीय, rt. वाञ्छ्
def: v.n. To wish (for), to desire.

ety: bāṅć˚ = Prk. वच्च(इ), fr. S. वाच्य
def: v.t. To read; to study; to decipher, make out.
more: v. a. S. वच् to speak. 1. To read; peruse ( paṛhnā ). 1. Kyā ilm unhoṅ ne sīkh līyā, jo bin likkhe ko bāṅche haiṅ, Aur bāt nahīṅ muṅh se niklī, bin hoṅṭ hilāë jāṅche haiṅ . Nazīr. What knowledge rare have they obtained who read what is not writ, No word nor movement of the lips, but all-discerning wit. 2. Kāgaz ho to bāṅch lūn, karam na bāṅchā jāë, Tiṅkā ho to toṛ dūn, prīt na toṛī jāë . Dohā. A written paper may be read, but fate who can e'er read? A slender stick may snap, but love's cords who can break indeed? 3. Pāti ādho milan hai, jo paūṅhche nīj pās, Bāṅchat hī sukh hot hai, lagi rahat nit ās . Dohā. A letter half a meeting is, whene'er it reaches one, It is such pleasure reading it—the diet hope's fed on. 2. To make out; decipher ( upāṛnā , 2.). 3. To pass over; overlook; disregard; neglect. Hin. Wom. Sab ko to nāryal diye, ek mere chhore ko, Rām jī rakkho, kyoṅ bāṅchā? Hin. Wom.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit वाचयति (vācayati, “causes to speak”). Compare Konkani वाजुचे (vājuce), वाच्चे (vācce), Gujarati વાંચવું (vā̃cvũ), Marathi वाचणे (vācṇe), Punjabi ਵਾਚਣਾ (vācṇā) / واچݨا (vācṇā).

def: v.n. To be read or perused.
more: v. n. S. वच् to speak. To be read ( paṛhā jānā ). baṅchvā'nā , v. a. Caus. of bānchnā , q. v.
v. n. To be read or perused.

def: v.t. To pass over, overlook, disregard, neglect; — also (prov.) = baćnā, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit वाचयति (vācayati, “causes to speak”). Compare Konkani वाजुचे (vājuce), वाच्चे (vācce), Gujarati વાંચવું (vā̃cvũ), Marathi वाचणे (vācṇe), Punjabi ਵਾਚਣਾ (vācṇā) / واچݨا (vācṇā).

def: v.n. To cause to be read, to have (a thing) read; (caus. of bāṅćnā), to cause to read, to make, or to hear (one) read.

ety: Pehl. band, Zend banda, S. बन्ध bandha
def: s.m. Binding, fastening, tie, knot, band, bandage, bond, ligature, belt, roll, string; joint, knuckle; stoppage, discontinuance; hindrance, prevention, obstacle; fetters, imprisonment, arrest, captivity, confinement; a mound, dyke, dam, embankment; gripe, hold, grasp, twist, trick (in wrestling); manœuvre, dodge, artifice; art, skill, dexterity; agreement, promise, pledge, contract; regulation; list, inventory; verse, stanza, burden (of a song); — (as an affix) binding; binder, fastener, putter on (e.g. naʻl-band, one who puts on horse-shoes, a farrier)
more: From P. بستن S वन्ध् to bind. A suffix, signifying an agent, as nāl-band a farrier. 1. Binding; fastening; bandage; bond. 2. Strings for fastening clothes ( tanī ). Cholī ke band darkan lāge, ab kaisī karūṅ, more Rām! Song. Birah. 3. A mound raised to preserve water for irrigation; a dam; dike; embankment. Har ghar meṅ is tarah se bhar āī hai muflisī, Pānī kā ṭūṭ jāve hai jūṅ ekbār band. Shahr-āshob . A ruined town. Nazīr. On every house fell poverty had laid its heavy hand, As prisoned waters burst their banks and overwhelm the land. 4. Stoppage; discontinuance; stagnancy. 5. Hindrance; prevention; bar; impediment; obstacle. 6. Custody; confinement; imprisonment; captivity. Band ke jāë band meṅ nahīṅ rahte . Prov. Not ever will his cell the prisoner retain. (Misfortune will not always cling to one). 7. Art; skill; dexterity; expertness. Woh log ek kauṛī ke muhtāj ab haiṅ, āh! Kasb o hun…

adj. 1. Prevented; barred; checked; stopped. Rāstā band hai . 2. Shut; bolted; locked up; closed. Daftar band hai . 3. Put out of caste; excommunicated. Unkā huqqā pānī band hai . His caste fellows have been interdicted from smoking, or drinking the same water with him. 4. Still; standing; quiescent; stagnant. 5. Choked; suffocated; stifled. 6. Hushed; lulled; silent. 7. Heavy; dull; sick at heart. Āj kuchh band band baiṭhe haiṅ . 8. Narrow; contracted; bow-legged. Ṭaṭṭū kī pichhlī ṭāṅgeṅ band haín . 9. Struck by paralysis; paralyzed. Ṭahlāo, nahīṅ ghoṛā band ho jāvegā. band haizā , n. m. Choleraic diarrhæa.
n. m. H. būṅd a drop. A drop; semen; sperma genetale ; blood. Pārā Mahādeo kā bind hai . Mercury is the sperm of Shiv.

s. m. f. A fastening, a knot, a bond, imprisonment, arrest, captivity, a bandage, belt, roll, string, joint, knuckle; a sort of poetical composition or ode. adj. Fastened, bound; shut, stopped. (In compos.) Bound, armed, or part. act. (of بستن) Binding, binder. بندخانه band-ḵẖāna, s. m. A prison. بندباندهنا band bāndhnā, To restrain an inundation with dykes. بندبندپکڙنا band band pakaṛnā, To seize (as rheumatism, &c.) all oneʼs joints. بندبيٿهنا band baiṭhnā, To succeed in an affair. بندپکڙنا band pakaṛnā, To object to the arguments of an opponent. بندرهنا band rahnā, To be engaged in a business. بندکرنا band karnā, To fasten, bind, imprison, confine, captivate, close, shut, stop, prevent. بندمين آنا band meṅ ānā or بندمين پڙنا band meṅ paṛnā, To become a captive.
s. A ligature, a mound raised round land to retain water for transplanting rice, &c.
subs: v.n. To construct a dam, dyke, embankment, &c.
s.f. Account of each share of assessment in grain
s.f. Account of the share of an instalment to be paid by each villager
s.m. Every joint; every part, every inch
band band judā karnā or kar-denā
(-kā), To sever all the joints, open all the knobs or fastenings, separate inch by inch
s.f. Statement of the amount of each money instalment of a village
v.n. lit. 'The joints to fit in'; to succeed in an affair
s.m. Account of the shares of the liabilities of a village
s.m. Damming up a watercourse for the purpose of irrigation
s.m. The act of allotting to each cultivator an equal portion of good and bad lands
s.m. Stated period for the settlement of an account
band-meṅ paṛnā, v.n. To fall into the toils; to become a captive

s.m. lit. 'Binding and fastening'; plan, organization, management, administration; arrangement, disposition, method, order, system; settlement (of revenue), regulation; — band-o-bast-i-ākhir, s.m. Recent or last settlement; — band-o-bast-i istimrārī, s.m. Permanent settlement; settlement in perpetuity; — band-o-bast-i ćand-roza, s.m. Temporary settlement; — band-o-bast-i-jadīd, band-o-bast-i ḥāl, s.m. New settlement; new regulation or practice
s.m. Permanent settlement ( = band-o-bast-i istimrārī); — band-o-bast-i-sar-sarī, s.m. = band-o-bast-i ćand-roza; — band-o-bast-i sābiq, s.m. Former settlement
(-kā), To manage, regulate, settle, make an arrangement, arrange (for), organize ( = intiz̤ām karnā, q.v.)
s.m. Sum total of the revenue of a province or kingdom
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَنْد (band).

ety: part. of bastan; Pehl. band, Z. banda; S. बद्ध, rt. बन्ध्
def: part. adj. Fastened, tied up, bound; shut, closed, stopped, stopped up, cut off; prevented, hindered, barred, checked, restrained, suppressed; constrained, still, dull, heavy, paralyzed; contracted, straitened
subs: v.n. To be engaged in a business
v.t. To shut, close, shut up, confine, imprison; to stop, stop up, fill up (a hole, &c.); to arrest, check, put a stop to, hinder, mar; to settle, wind up (an account)
or ho-jānā, v.n. To be closed, stopped, cut off, shut up, stopped up; to be finished, be exhausted, to cease; to be abolished, be done away, be lost or gone.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَنْد (band).

def: (dakh.) To betroth.

v. n. See بند adj. (1, 2) and بندکرنا v. a. (2 to 9). 1. To be stopped, shut up, closed (a trade). Kuchh ek do ke kām kā ronā nahīṅ hai, yār, Chhattīs peshe-wāloṅ kā hai kār o bār band. Shahr-āshob . A ruined-town. Nazīr. Tis not the grief of one or two one mourns, my friend, But six and thirty trades shut up one's heart must rend. 2. To be finished; to expire; come to an end. Āj mahīnā band hai . 3. To be abolished, closed, given up. Madarsā band ho gayā . 4. To be over, or exhausted. Ām āne band ho gaë . 5. To die; die out; expire; cease to exist. 6. To be excommunicated, put out of caste. Birādrī meṅ uskā ānā jānā band ho gayā . 7. To become blunt (as a knife). Kyā bāt ek bāl kaṭe yā tarāshe kor, Yāṅ tak hai ustre o naharnī kī dhār band . Nazīr. Razors and scissors grown so blunt they're quite unfit A single hair to cut or nails to pare a bit.

v. a. 1. To shut; shut up; pen; coop; bolt; lock up or in; encage; close; seal up. 2. To confine; imprison; incarcerate. 3. To fill in or up; choke; stop. Is gaṛhe ko band kar do . 4. To choke; stifle; suffocate. Dam band kar dīyā . 5. To arrest; stop; put a stop to. Phr. To stop the way; put a spoke in the wheel. Ātā hai aise hāl pe ronā hameṅ to, hāë! Dushman kā bhī Khudā na kare kār o bār band! Shahr-āshob . A ruined town. Nazīr. Their state so sad, alas! I cannot do but weep, Even an enemy from want of food God keep! 6. To still; hush; lull; silence; gag. Khalaq kā halaq kis ne band kīyā hai ? Prov. Who can stop the people's voice? 7. To silence; confute; defeat; beat. Dam dam meṅ band karte haiṅ, nāsah, tujhe to log, Par tū woh be-hayā hai ki suntā zarā nahīṅ . Saīyad. So oft, O moralist, you've silenced been, but yet you prate, You do not heed a bit, so shameless and infatuate! 8. To frustrate; thwart; balk; spoil. 9. To spoil or render ineffective by magic; defeat by a charm. Tomṛī band kar dī . 10. (Chess.) To give a check to the king. Chāl band karnā . 11. To dismiss; send away; dispense with. Madad band kar dī . 12. To leave off; discontinue; relinquish; give over. Wahāṅ kā ānā jānā band kar diyā . 13. To strike a balance; settle an account. Hisāb band karnā .
s.m. Name of a place in Bundelkhand.
more: Tir. n. m. S. वन्दा A parasite; Cymbidium tessalloides . Wat.

n. m. S. वण्ड maimed. A tailless bird, ox, or serpent. bāṅ'ḍā , adj. 1. Tailless; docked; lopped. 2. Lame; limping. Halve halve aisā chāle jūṅ chāle mānas bāṅḍā . W. Rus. Song. He creeps along as though he had lost his tail.
s. m. A parasite plant which grows on trees. (Epidendrum, particularly tesselatum. Roxb. ).

def: s.m. The parasitical plant Epidendrum tesselatum; a parasitical plant (syn. ākāś-vela).
adj. & s.m. Maimed, crippled; docked; tailless; — a tailless bird; a tailless snake; a man who has been circumcised (applied as a term of abuse to Musalmans, especially in Bengal).
more: adj. Tail-less. s. m. A serpent which has lost its tail.

def: s.m. Mistletoe, Epidendron teselloides ( = bāṅdā, q.v.). s.m. Name of a place in Bundelkhand.
more: n. m. Mistletoe; a kind of epidendron; Epidendron tesselloides , Roxb.
s. m. Mistletoe, a kind of epidendron. (Epidendrum tesselatum). See باندا.
(for بنده q. v.) s. m. A slave.

def: s.m. A grain magazine above ground (cf. bādār).
s.m. = P. بنده banda, q.v.
s.m. Mistletoe, &c. ( = bandā, q.v.). s.m. Name of a place in Bundelkhand.
more: s. m. Mistletoe, a kind of epidendron. See بندا bandā.
s. m. (dakh.) Stone, a great stone, rock, cliff ( Harris ).

def: s.m. Slave, bondman, servant; humble servant (a term used by a speaker of himself, when addressing a superior); human being, creature
more: s. m. f. Binding, bandage, bondage, imprisonment. بندهـ مين پڙناياآنا bandh meṅ paṛnā yā ānā, To become a captive.
subs: s.m. Cherisher of servants or dependants, patron; your honour, your worship
s.m. Your slave's son, my son

def: s.m. pl. (of banda), Slaves, servants.
s.f. Slavery, servitude; service; devotion, adoration, worship, praise; compliment, salutation; humility, lowliness; — intj. My service to you! good morning! good-bye! adieu! farewell! thank you!
more: n. f. P. band , S. बन्ध् to bind. 1. Salutation; compliment. Gar ko merī bandgī, koṭ koṭ parnām, Saṅkkaṭ kabhū na byāpye, paṛe na jam se kām . Dohā. My salutations to my priest ten million million times, Keep me from hardships and sharp pains, from Pluto save betimes! 2. Humility; lowliness; self-abasement; meekness. 3. Worship; adoration; homage; devotion. 4. Service; servitude; employment. Rare . 1. Bandgī be-chārgī . Prov. Service is helplessness. 2. Jaisī bandgī, vaisā inām . Prov. The reward is as the service. bandgī , intj. 1. Farewell; adieu; good-bye. Aisī mulāqāt ko bandagī! A truce to such intercourse! 2. Thanks; thank you. 3. Why not; bravo; very fine; any thing else you would like. bandgī bajānā , v. n. To serve; place one's services at another's disposal.
s. f. Slavery, service, devotion, compliment. بندگي مين خداٴي کرنا bandagī meṅ ḵẖudā-ī karnā, To affect authority while in servitude.
s. (dakh.) A mode of salutation: see Qanoon-e-islam, gloss. p. xcvii.
subs: v.n. To affect authority while in servitude.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَنْدَگی (bandagē).

def: n. m. P. bastan , S. bandh to bind. 1. A slave; a bondsman; a servant; a term expressing self-abasement in addressing a superior ( banda-i-dargāh ). 2. A human being or creature; a man. Āyā bandah āī rozī, gayā bandah g /?/ū rozī . Prov. Come man come food, gone man gone food. (With the birth of the child the mother's milk begins to flow). bandah-parwar, bandah-nawāz , n. m. 1. A patron; a cherisher of servants or dependants. Be-niyāzī had se guzrī, bandah-parwar, kab-talak Ham kaheṅge hāl-i-dil, aur āp farmāveṅge 'kyā?' Unkindness to excess has grown, how long, my lady, pray? My heart's grief I lay bare, you only say, 'what did you say?' 2. "Your worship;" "your honor."
more: s. m. A slave, a servant, bondsman. بنده پرور banda-parwar, Cherisher of servants (a respectful expression used in addressing a superiour; as, your honour!). بنده پروري banda-parwar-ī, s. f. Cherishing of servants. بندۀدرگاه banda-i-dargāh, The slave of (your) threshold. بنده زاده banda-zāda, Son of (your) slave, i. e. my son. بنده نواز banda-nawāz, Cherisher of servants or dependants, patron. (Pers. plur. بندگان or بندها).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَنْدَه (banda). By surface analysis, بَنْد (band, “close; block; confined”) + ـَہ (-a). Doublet of بَانْدْھنَا (bāndhnā, “to tie”). First attested in c. 1649 as Middle Hindi بندہ (banda). * Sense 5 is a semantic loan from Punjabi بَنْدَہ /ਬੰਦਾ (bandah, “person; man”).

def: s.m. pl. (of banda), Slaves, servants.
s.m. Fastening, &c. ( = bandhan, q.v.) s.m. Fixed allowance, wages, salary, stipend, pension; raised embankments of earth for flooding lands; purchase of grain in advance of harvest.
s.m. Praise, worship, adoration; obeisance, homage, salutation; flattery
subs: (S. vandana+mālā), s.f. A festoon of leaves and flowers suspended across gateways on festive occasions.

def: s. f. Cherishing of servants.
(from बंधन Binding वार Door-way, gate), and s. f. A wreath or garland of leaves and flowers suspended across gateways on marriages or publick festivals.
s.f. See s.v. bandan. (The word is sometimes incorrectly written bandhan-wār, as though derived from bandhana and dwāra.)
more: (from बंधन Binding वार Door-way, gate), and s. f. A wreath or garland of leaves and flowers suspended across gateways on marriages or publick festivals.

def: s.m. Monkey, ape
more: n. m. A port; harbour; emporium. shāh-bandar , n. m. Chief port.

n. m. S. वानर from वन forest, नर man, or (?) वा like, नर man. A monkey; ape; babboon. 1. Bandar bandaryā chābe pān, Uṛ gaī chuṭyā, rah gaë kān . Boys' rhyme. The monkey's lips are red with chewing pan, Her two great ears remain, her top-knot gone. 2. Bandar kī āshnāī kyā? Prov. What is a monkey's friendship worth? (He is so treacherous). 3. Bandar ke hāth nāryal! Prov. A cocoanut in a monkey's hand! (Something unappreciated). 4. Kūkar, bāṅdar, chhokrā, chauthe bidhwā nār, In se kabhī na bolye, chauṛe kareṅ bigāṛ . Dohā. A dog, a monkey, and a boy, and fourth a widow too, Ne'er say a word to these, they'll shame you 'fore the public view.
s. m. A city, an emporium, a port, harbour, a trading town to which numbers of foreign merchants resort.
subs: (S. vānara+kshata), s.m. A sore that does not heal (as a monkey-bite); a running sore, an issue.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بندر (bandar). Compare Bengali বন্দর (bondor), Dhivehi ބަނދަރު (baⁿdaru).

def: s. m. A rocket-thrower.
s.m. Inlet (of the sea), harbour, anchorage, sea-port, emporium, city, trading town (on the sea) to which numbers of foreign merchants resort.

ety: bāṅdh˚ = Prk. बंध(इ) = S. बध्ना(ति), rt. बन्ध्
def: v.t. To bind, tie, fix, fasten; to tie up, tighten; to bind up, dress (hair, &c.); to fasten or tie (on or round), bind (round), put (on, -par; e.g. ghāʼo-par pulṭis bāṅdho); to chain, enchain, fetter; to take captive, enthral, captivate, bring under (one's) influence; to shut up, confine, imprison; to fix, direct, fasten (hope, eyes, aim, &c. upon, -par); to shut, close, stop (water, &c.), to embank; to arrest, hold back, check; to bind together, join together (in marriage, &c.), fasten together, put together, join, connect, conglomerate, unite, gather, pack, set (e.g. gathrī bāṅdhnā; haḍḍī bāṅdhnā; hāth bāṅdhnā; laḍḍū bāṅdhnā; maẓmūn bāṅdhnā); to build, construct (dam, bridge, &c.); to compose (verses); to form, produce, make, constitute (e.g. ṣaf bāṅdhnā; nīyat bāṅdhnā; samā bāṅdhnā); to entertain, cherish, foster, have, contract, form (enmity, friendship, &c.); to put on, lay on, impose, levy (e.g. maḥsūl bāṅdhnā); to put on, lay (a stake, wager, &c.); to fix, erect, set up (a post, standard, &c.); to devise, contrive, plan; to make (of, -ko), constitute, regard as, compare to (e.g. tū usko tār bāṅdh, 'you regard her as, or liken her to, a palm')
more: v. a. S. बन्ध् to bind, P. band . 1. To tie; fasten; tether; gird; bind (a book). 2. To bind or join together; marry. 3. To pack together; roll or pack up; make a bundle. Gaṭhṛī bāṅdhnā . 4. To link; hook; chain. 5. To weave; plait; braid; dress (the hair). Sir bāṅdhnā . 6. To set (a bone). Joṛ, yā haḍḍī bāṅdhnā . 7. To put or clap on; apply. Pulṭis bāṅdhnā . 8. To join; fold (one's hands). Hāth bāṅdhnā . 9. To form line; parade; arrange in rows. Saf bāṅdhnā . 10. To build; construct; raise; throw across (a dam or bridge). Pānī, yā pul bāṅdhnā . 11. To form; fashion; shape; mould; lump together. Laḍḍū bāṅdhnā . 12. To make; make up; constitute. Āṅsuoṅ kā tār bāṅdh dīyā . 13. To make a good scene or picture; produce harmony. Samā bāṅḍhnā . 14. To chain; impound; put in custody; imprison ( qaid k .). Ek sāl ko bāṅdh dīyā . 15. To compose; put or string together. Mazmūn bāṅdhnā . 16. To rev…
v. a. To bind, tie, fasten, shut, stop water, embank; to invent, contrive, devise, stop, pack, aim, build, compose, form, put, settle.
subs: v.t. (intens. of bāṅdhnā), To bind up, tie up, &c.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀩𑀁𑀥𑀇 (baṃdhaï), from Sanskrit बन्ध॑ति (bándhati), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *bʰándʰati, from Proto-Indo-European *bʰéndʰ-e-ti, from *bʰendʰ- (“to bind, tie”). Cognate with Punjabi بَنّھݨا (bannhṇā) / ਬੰਨ੍ਹਣਾ (bannhṇā), Saraiki ٻَنھَݨ (ḇanhaṇ), Sindhi ٻَنڌَڻُ (ḇãdhaṇu) / ॿंधणु, Marathi बांधणे (bāndhṇe), English bind.

def: v.n. To be fastened, bound, tied, kept; to be enclosed, be closed or stopped, be laid; to be caught, be ensnared; to be formed or composed; to be continuous or uninterrupted, be fixed or established; to be conducive, to help, co-operate; — s.m. Instrument for tying, tie, rope, &c. ( = bandhnī, q.v.); cloth for wrapping small articles in, wrapper; needle-case; hold-all
more: n. m. 1. A wrapper; a cloth for keeping small articles in. 2. A needle case; a housewife; a hold-all.

v. n. See باندهنا v. a. baṅdhā-huā, baṅdhī-huī , adj. Established; fixed; usual; ordinary. baṅdhā-huā kharch , n. m. Regular or usual expenses. baṅdhī muṭṭhī, baṅdhī buhārī (a faggot), n. f. 1. An undisclosed intention; a secret. 2. A compact and united family. Baṅdhī muṭṭhī lākh barābar . Prov. A closed fist counts for a lakh . ☞ The value of the present made with the closed fist is exaggerated by the receiver who wants to show how highly he was appraised. baṅdhī muṭṭhī , adv. Obs. Peaceably; quietly. Zabāṅ rakh gunche sāṅ apne dahan meṅ, Baṅdhī muṭṭhī chalā jā is chaman meṅ . Mīr. Your tongue keep in your mouth, a bud as yet unblown, And silently this world go through, your heart unshown.
v. n. To be tied, to be fastened, to be bound, to be continuous or uninterrupted, to be kept, to be enclosed, to be laid, to be formed or composed. بندهي موٿهي bandhī mūṭhī, An undisclosed intention.
1. v. n. To be closed or stopped. 2. v. a. (dakh.) See باندهنا bāndhnā.
subs: v.n. To be formed, be composed, &c. = bandhnā.

def: s.m. Invention, fabrication; slander, calumny; plan, plot; story, fiction, romance; a mode of dying in which the cloth is tied up in different places to prevent the parts tied from receiving the dye; a checked or spotted cloth; a kind of coloured silk (syn. gul-badan); a species of parrot
more: n. m. 1. A fabrication; invention; falsehood; fib. 2. False charge; libel; calumny; slander. 3. A plan; scheme; design; plot. 4. A story; fable; romance; fiction. 5. A picture of the imagination; a flight of fancy. 6. A spotted, or checked cloth. 7. The act of tying up portions of cloth which are not intended to receive a particular dye. bāṅdhnū bāṅdhnā , v. n. To invent; fabricate; trump up.
s. m. 1. A mode of dying, in which the cloth is tied in different places to prevent the parts tied from receiving the die. 2. Slander, plot, plan. 3. A kind of silk cloth. 4. A kind of parrot.

The priest a (greedy) goat, the pupil is the cotton, The goat comes to the house and eats up all the cotton. ☞ At every visit the pupil has to make him a present. 2. Bāṅgā meṅ siyār gaile, kā oṛh aile, kā penh aile! Mag. Prov. A jackal in a cotton-field, what wore he, what put on! (Cotton is of no use to the jackal).

Kahīṅ retī meṅ baṅglā chhavāo, khilāṛī, jahāṅ āve laher Jamnā kī . Wom. Song. Build me, my sportive love, a cottage on a sandy plain, washed by the Jamnā waves.
s. m. 1. A kind of thatched house, a summerhouse. 2. A sort of betel leaf or pān so called.

For one who's rich a rich bed is bespread and decked, A mistress fair beside with sparkling bangles decked. 2. Lo chūṛī baṅgṛī! Bangle-sellers' cry.
s. f. (in the dakhan ) A ring of coloured glass worn on the wrist by women ( Qanoon-e-islam, app. p. xxv).

def: s.f. The arm (from the elbow to the shoulder); sleeve; support, help; supporter, protector; security, guarantee (as when a person trusts himself in the power of an enemy, a third person, who engages to return him to his home, &c. in security, is his bāṅh); power, strength
more: n. f. S. वाहु, Pālī, bāhā . 1. The arm; the sleeves. Ṭūṭī bāṅh gale paṛe . Prov. The broken arm hangs from the neck. (The loss of the bread-winner lays a burden on the family. 2. Help; help; support. 3. A supporter ( bāzū , 2). Bāṅh bal, na zar bal . Prov. Nor men nor money. 4. Guarantee; security, as when a person trusts himself in the power of an enemy, a third person being his bāṅh who engages to restore him to his house or fort in security. 5. Strength; vigour; power. bāṅh buland honā , v. n. To be powerful, munificent. Bhagwān kī bāṅh buland hai, chāhe to chhappar phāṛ ke de . Prov. Great is God's far-reaching arm, if it please Him he can pour down a shower of wealth through this thatched roof. bāṅh-bal , n. m. 1. Strength of arm. 2. A full brother. 3. Support; ally. bāṅh pakaṛnā, yā gahnā , v. a. To take under one's protection; to support; patronize. 1. Baṛe na būṛan det hain, jā k…

s. f. 1. The arm. 2. Guarantee, protector or security (as, when a person trusts himself in the power of an enemy, a third person, who engages to return him to his house or fort in security, is his bāṅh ). بانهـ پکڙنا bāṅh pakaṛnā, To protect. بانهـ ٿوٿنا bāṅh ṭūṭnā, To be destitute of friends or protectors. بانهـ دينا bāṅh denā, To assist: see بازودينا bāzū denā. بانهـ گهنا bāṅh gahnā, To protect. بانهين چڙهانا bāṅheṅ chaṛhānā, To get ready, to prepare.

more: n. f. S. वाणी 1. Voice; speech; language; word. Koyal bole imrat bānī . 2. Precept; dogma; tenet; doctrine. 3. Sectarian verses of mendicants. Ḍanḍā le-ke tujhe maiṅ bānī khūb sunāī, Dekhūṅ, Lālā, kaisā bishnī, bhīk merī aṭkāī? Sutre Shāhī. Mendicants. 4. The goddess of all learning ( Saraswati ).

n. m. بني He built it. 1. Builder; author; parent; founder; originator; framer; composer; inventor. 2. Source; root; spring; main-spring. 3. Instigator; beginner; prime mover. bānī-i-fasād , n. m. Ringleader; instigator; aggressor. bānī-kār , adj. Very sharp; clever; adroit; expert. Jitne naukar haiṅ uske khidmatgār, Fan-i-duzdī meṅ sab haiṅ bānī-kār . A satire on the city Kotwāl . Saudā. And all the servants in his house, For larceny have a special nous. bānī-kār , n. m. A sharper; a clever rogue; a consummate knave.
part. act. (from بني) used substantively, A builder, architect, composer, author, inventor, founder.
s. f. 1. The price paid for a work. 2. Ashes. 3. The thread with which cloth is woven. (Sans. वाणि Weaving).
s. f. Speech, voice, language; name of the goddess Saraswatī.

ety: S. बणिज्; or बणिज्य for बाणिज
def: s.m. Trade, traffic, commerce, mercantile transactions, merchandise
more: E., n. m. S. बाणिज्य. Buying and selling; traffic; trade; commerce; barter; exchange; dealings; transactions; merchandise. 1. Maryo, iskā bāp nikhaṭṭū, ke iskā dukh gāūṅ rī, Khetī kare, na banje jāve, maiṅ din din samjhāūṅ rī . Rus. Wom. Song. May his father die—a wretch who will not earn— to whom complain? He'll neither till nor trade, each day I urge him all in vain. 2. Bābal bhejī banaj ko, gaī ḍagaryā bhūl, Ṭhagwā mo ko mil gayo, lābh rahyo, nā mūl . Dohā. My father sent me cut to trade, but soon I lost my way, I fell in with some thieves, my stock and gain both snatched away. banaj, yā behvār karnā , v. a. To trade; traffic. Banaj kareṅge bānye, aur kareṅge rīs, Banaj karā thā Jāṭ ne, rah gaë sau ke tīs . Prov. To trade a banyā understands, but others come to grief, A Jāt once took to trade, to thirty came his hundred lief.
s. m. Trade, traffick, merchandise, commerce.

Banyā jiskā yār, usko dushman kyā darkār? Prov. A banyā for your fellow, you need no other foe. 3. Aiṅtvār jab jānye jab haṭṭī līpeṅ bānye . Prov. When shops being plastered are, it's Sunday in the Calendar. 2. A miser ( kanjūs ); a niggard. 3. ( Ironic .) A timid person; a coward. Mujhe bhī koī banyā samjhā hai!

more: n. f. The wife, or tent of a banjārā. banjā'rī , adj. 1. Belonging or related to banjārās . 2. Half-boiled (grain).
(see بنجارا) s. f. A kind of tent used by banjārās. 2. adj. f. Half-boiled (grain).

def: s.f. Wife of a banjārā; a kind of tent used by banjārās; half-boiled grain.
adj. Barren (woman); unproductive, sterile (soil).

(land) adj. S. बंध्या. Barren; unproductive; sterile (soil or woman). 1. Jane jane kā man rakhte beswā paṛ gaī bāṅjh . Prov. Through pleasing every one, the harlot's barren grown. 2. Bāṅgar boyā bājṛā, khādar boye dhān, Āp ni-pūtā hījṛā, mohe batāwe bāṅjh . Wom. Song. Relū . On high land bājra sown and rice on low for water pant, The wretched impotent says I am barren—lor' he cant. 3. Gunī kā shram gunī hī paichhāne, Bāṅjh kahā parasūti dukh jāne? Bihārī Lāl. The toil of reaching excellence the masters only know, What can the barren woman of the pains of childbirth know?
more: n. m. A hill tree. The natives usually make necklaces of the fruit for their children, as a charm against evil spirits.

def: s.m. The tree Quercus incana, or dilatata (common in the outer Himalaya, the acorns of which are commonly made into necklaces for children, and are regarded as a charm against evil spirits).
n. f. A nostrum believed to cause barrenness.
Pill or bolus given to destroy the embryo; medicine taken to produce barrenness.
more: s. f. A medicine taken to produce barrenness.

def: s.m. (?), Land close by a village
subs: (about three feet high) which springs up with the ḵẖarīf crops.

def: s.m. Crookedness, curvature; crook, curve, bend, winding, sweep; bend or reach of a river, winding course of a stream; craftiness, duplicity; wickedness, offence, fault; — a hook or curved instrument to cut bamboos and sugar-cane with ( = bāṅkā); a dagger with a curved blade (in this and the following senses the word is usually fem.); a semicircular armlet (syn. bāzū); a kind of anklet; a settee; exercise with the dagger
more: n. m. S. वङ्क् to be crooked. 1. Crook; curve; curvature; deflexion; bend; bow; horse-shoe; curl; winding; sweep. 2. A crook to cut bamboos or sugarcane with. 3. A dagger having a curved blade. 4. An armlet (Mah. Wom.); anklet (Hin. Wom.). bāṅk-paṭā , n. m. Fencing with wooden dagger and cutlass.

1. s. m. A crook, curvature, bending. 2. A reach or turning of a river. 3. Fault, offence, wickedness. 4. s. f. A semicircular ornament worn on the arms. 5. A kind of dagger. 6. An exercise with the dagger. 7. A settee.
subs: s.m. A method of fencing with a (wooden) dagger and cutlass.

def: s.m. Bank
more: s. m. A bending, a curvature, the reach or bend of a river. ¶ banik ( वणिक् ) s. m. A merchant, a trader.

def: s.m. Bending, curvature; bend, elbow, or reach of a river or winding stream.

adj. & s.m. Crooked, winding, curved, bent, awry, on one side; oblique, tortuous, retrograde (motion of planets); crooked in disposition, cunning, fraudulent; malevolent, refractory, rebellious; — foppish, coxcombish, dashing, showy, smart, gay, coquettish, wanton; fine; spirited; — a fop, beau, buck, dandy; bravo, bully; dissolute fellow, rake, roué (syn. albelā); — a hook or curved instrument to cut bamboos, &c. with ( = bāṅk); a particular musical instrument, a bent trumpet made of brass
more: adj. S. वक्र, Pr. vankam crooked. 1. Bent; curved; crooked; ( Math .) oblique. 2. Turned against; malcontent; refractory; revolted. Zabān shīrīṅ, mulk gīrī, Zabān ṭeṛhī, mulk bāṅkā . Prov. With sweet words you all hearts may sway, But harsh ones all will turn away. 3. Foppish; coxcombical; mincing; gallant. 1. Daīyā, chaṭak, chhabīlā, bāṅkā, tarwaryā, Mo se nain milāwat bār bār . Song. Thumrī . O nurse, a dashing soldier debonair I met, Such amorous glances o'er and o'er on me he set. 2. Thāḍā rahīyo, ve bāṅke yār, gāgar maiṅ ghar dharī āiūṅ . Wom. Song. Ṭhumrī . Wait here, my handsome love, the waterpot set down I will return. 4. Fine; exquisite; crack; tip-top. 5. Spirited; dashing; valorous. Yā ho sipāhī bāṅkā, tirchhā, baṛā kahāyā, Bal-dār bāṅdh chīrā, turre ko jagmagāyā, Khetoṅ meṅ jā-ke kūdā, lākhoṅ ke taīṅ bhagāyā, Jab muṅh ajal kā dekhā, phir kuchh bhī ban na āyā . On death. Nazī…

n. m. bāṅkī , n. f. 1. A dandy; rake ( albelā , 2 to 4). Terī jurvā kī ṭāṅgoṅ meṅ to khishtak bhī nahīṅ sābit, Muë tuf! tū banā phirtā hai galyoṅ meṅ kaṛak bāṅkā . Wom. Rāhat. Your wife has not a pair of trousers whole, out on you, wretch! Got up a swell, you strut in every lane in hopes to fetch. 2. A dissolute, licentious fellow; a debauchee. bāṅkā chor , n. m. An expert or clever thief.
s. m. 1. A fop, a beau, a coxcomb, a buck, a bravo, a bully. 2. A knife or hook to cut bamboo with. 3. s. f. Name of a river. 4. adj. Crooked, foppish, coxcombish, arrant.
s. m. (dakh.) A bended trumpet made of brass ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: s. m. A fop, a beau, a coxcomb, a bully, a bravo.

s.f. Bend, &c. = baṅk, q.v. s.m. Crookedness, curvature; crook, curve, bend, winding, sweep; bend or reach of a river, winding course of a stream; craftiness, duplicity; wickedness, offence, fault; — a hook or curved instrument to cut bamboos and sugar-cane with ( = bāṅkā); a dagger with a curved blade (in this and the following senses the word is usually fem.); a semicircular armlet (syn. bāzū); a kind of anklet; a settee; exercise with the dagger
subs: s.m. A method of fencing with a (wooden) dagger and cutlass.

def: s. f. A bending, curvature, reach or bend of a river.
s.m. Beau, fop, &c. = bāṅkā.

s.f. Foppishness, &c. = bāṅkpan, q.v. s.m. Crookedness, curvature, &c.; foppishness, coxcombry, dandyism, smartness, gayness, elegance; skittishness, wantonness; disorderly conduct, rakishness, debauchery.

s.m. See s.v. ban, 'forest.' s.f. Quality, property, temper, disposition, nature, character, conduct, manners, habit, custom (cf. ān-bān, s.v. ān); — s.m. The marriage rite which prescribes a certain number of baths (generally from three to eleven) for the bride and bridegroom
subs: v.n. To take the baths prescribed by the marriage rite (mentioned above)
v.n. To fall into, or to acquire or form, a habit, &c.; to be trained; to be accustomed (to)
v.n. To lay the foundation of character, &c., cultivate a habit, &c.; to educate, train; to habituate, accustom, inure; to naturalize

def: part. (of bannā), Having become; — conj. & adv. As, like (often used as synonymous with hokar, when it either has the sense of 'as,' or gives to the preceding word the force of an adverb; e.g. mitra bankar mile jule raho, Live together as friends in peace and harmony; āp nir-daʼī ban taṭṭū-par ćalā jātā hai, He himself is going heartlessly along upon the pony).
v.n. To cry out, shout, yell, bellow, roar, thunder; to make answer (as one possessed by an evil spirit).
more: v. n. S. वच to speak. 1. To roar; yell; thunder. 2. To make answer or reply (one possessed by an evil spirit).

def: s.m. = baṅg-katiyā, q.v.s.v. bang. s.f. Voice, sound, noise, cry, shout; the call to prayer by the muʼaẕẕin from the minarets or towers of the mosques (syn. aẕān); crowing (of a cock)
subs: v.n. To call Musalmāns to prayer; to crow (a cock)
s.f. The morning call to prayer
v.n. To cry out; to call
s.f. The call to prayer ( = bāṅg).

def: n. m. H. kāṅṭā thorn. A prickly plant resembling a thistle, used medicinally for checking diarrhœa. ban-goṅṭhā, ban-kanḍā , n. m. S. वनगोविट Dry cow-dung found in forests.

part. (of bannā) = bankar, q.v. s.m. See s.v. ban, 'forest.' s.f. Quality, property, temper, disposition, nature, character, conduct, manners, habit, custom (cf. ān-bān, s.v. ān); — s.m. The marriage rite which prescribes a certain number of baths (generally from three to eleven) for the bride and bridegroom

def: s. m. A forest.
s. m. Name of a wood or forest.

def: s.m. Crookedness, curvature, &c.; foppishness, coxcombry, dandyism, smartness, gayness, elegance; skittishness, wantonness; disorderly conduct, rakishness, debauchery.
more: n. m. 1. Dandyism; foppery; coxcombry; man-millinery. 2. Gaiety; skittishness; airs; smartness; winsomeness; elegance ( albelā-pan , 3 to 8).

def: (بانکا and پن) s. m. Foppishness, debauchery, disorderly conduct.
s. m. A kind of fencing with (wooden) dagger and cutlass.
s.m. A ruined or spoiled crop (on the field).

n. f. A garland of wild flowers reaching to the feet, composed of tulsī ( ocymum sanctum ), kundā ( jasminum multiflorum ), mandār ( asclepias gigantea ), pārijāt ( Erythrinā fulgens ), and saroruha lotus.
more: s. f. A garland of various flowers reaching to the feet: usually of the flowers of the tulsī (Ocymum sanctum), kunda (Jasminum multiflorum), mandār (Asclepias gigantea), pārijāta (Erythrina fulgens), and lotus.

def: n. m. One who weaves a ban-māl ; an epithet of Krishna. ban-mānas , n. m. A wild man of the woods ( jaṅglī ādmī ); an orangoutang.
more: s. m. A name of Kr̤ish̤ṇa.

def: s. m. A man of the woods, a wild man, an orang-ūtan.
v.n. See بننا bannā.
more: v. n. S. वर्ण Pr. vanno to color. 1. To be made, produced, created, invented. 2. To be done, performed, managed, executed, effected, formed. 1. Bane jis taur se tum usko lānā, Dayā ko chhoṛ tum hargiz mat ānā . Dayā Rām kā kaṛkā. 2. Jidhar ko jāo, udhar gam jigar ko hai khātā, Ajab kharābī hai, kuchh, hāë! ban nahīṅ ātā . Nazīr. Whichever way one turns, black grief one's liver eats away, O ruin dire, alas! no salve our misery can allay. 3. To be set up, instituted, established, constituted. Ab to unke hāṅ bhī sawārī ban gaī . Even he has set up his carriage now. 4. To be; become; turn to. 1. Yār bannā . 2. Beṭā ban-ke sab ne khāyā hai, bāp ban-ke kisī ne nahīṅ . Prov. Say, you're to him a son as't were, and you'll be clothed and fed, But call yourself his father and not one will give you bread. 5. To stand; exist; subsist; live. Bābā, bane raho! Mendicant's Benediction. 6. To be present;…

Māṛ. n. m. 1. A bridegroom ( dūlhā ). 1. Behtar haiṅ muflisī meṅ, mīyāṅ, chābne chane, Jo tan-durast haiṅ wohī dūlah haiṅ aur bane . Nazīr. Better, my friend, be poor, with just parched gram for food, Who health enjoy are bridegrooms blessed with every good. 2. Gūṅdh lā, mālan, phūloṅ kā sehrā, Āj banā hove banī ko mubārak ! Marriage song. Sehrā . Wreathe, gardener fair, a crown of flowers so fair to see, Now may the bridegroom to the bride a blessing be! 2. A darling. 3. A marriage song.

v. n. 1. To be made, to be prepared, to be mended or adjusted. 2. To chime, to agree, to fall in with, to do, to answer, to serve, to be, to become, to avail, to counterfeit, to succeed. 3. s. m. A bridegroom. بنابنايا banā banāyā, Finished, ready made, complete, entire, perfect. بناچنا banā chunā, Dressed, decked out, arranged. بناٿهنا bannā ṭhannā, To be fully adorned or prepared, to be decked out. بنارهنا banā rahnā, To remain waiting.
(from بنا) s. m. A mason, a builder.
ex: انسان پانی کے بنا جی نہیں سکتے
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀺𑀡𑀸 (viṇā), from Sanskrit विना (vínā). Doublet of وِنَا (vinā), a tatsama. Cognate with Punjabi ਬਿਨ (bin) / بِن (bin), Mewari वना (vanā) Kachchi વિણ- (viṇa-), Marathi बिन- (bin-).

def: s.m. Mason, builder.
ex: انسان پانی کے بنا جی نہیں سکتے
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀺𑀡𑀸 (viṇā), from Sanskrit विना (vínā). Doublet of وِنَا (vinā), a tatsama. Cognate with Punjabi ਬਿਨ (bin) / بِن (bin), Mewari वना (vanā) Kachchi વિણ- (viṇa-), Marathi बिन- (bin-).

ety: ban˚ = Prk. वण(इ) = S. वन्य(ते), pass. of rt. वन्
def: v.n. To be made, constructed, built; to be created, formed, fashioned, produced, fabricated, invented; to be prepared, got ready, be done, finished or completed; to be cooked or dressed; to be managed, executed, effected; to be composed; to be mended, repaired, or adjusted: to be improved, be made presentable, palatable, &c.; to be established, be set up; to be, become; to happen, befall, betide; to be as, become as or like (see ban-kar); to be adorned, decorated, embellished; to fit, fit in, come out, come right; to chime, agree, fall in (with), do, answer, serve; to succeed, do well, prosper; to be made, acquired, or gained; to be assumed, be feigned, to counterfeit; to be hoaxed, be ridiculed or laughed at
subs: ban-parnā, v.n. To be performed, effected, or accomplished; to do; to succeed, turn out well, to answer, suit; to be fortunate, to prosper; to come to pass
v.n. Intens. of, and = bannā, q.v.
v.n. To be decked out, be thoroughly got up, or elaborately dressed and adorned.

def: s.m. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts.
more: s. m. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts.

def: s.f. Portion of grain given to a labourer as a remuneration for his services.
more: + Wom.; 1. A bride ( dulhan ). 2. A darling. 1. Terī kahtī hai banī, 'tujh ko maiṅ chāhūṅ kyā? Dāṛhī aisī hai terī rūī kā jaise gālā .' Saudā. (The young bride to her octagenarian husband). 'What's there in you to love', the bride thus to the bridegroom spoke, 'Say, is this skein of cotton white, your beard, a thing to stroke?' 2. Nahā dho-ke us roz aisī banī, Ki do din kī sach-much ho jaisī banī . Mīr Hasan. She washed and cleaned and got herself up so that day, Just like a new bride beautiful she looked, you'd say. 3. Banno, terā āyā banṛā, tū khol ghūṅghaṭ, mukh dekh . Song. Banṛā . Fair bride, your bridegroom's come, lift up your veil, his face behold.

def: s.f. Lady, &c. = bānū, q.v.; — A proper name. s.f. Lady; woman of rank, gentlewoman.

s.m. See s.v. ban, 'forest.' s.f. Quality, property, temper, disposition, nature, character, conduct, manners, habit, custom (cf. ān-bān, s.v. ān); — s.m. The marriage rite which prescribes a certain number of baths (generally from three to eleven) for the bride and bridegroom
subs: v.n. To take the baths prescribed by the marriage rite (mentioned above)
v.n. To fall into, or to acquire or form, a habit, &c.; to be trained; to be accustomed (to)
v.n. To lay the foundation of character, &c., cultivate a habit, &c.; to educate, train; to habituate, accustom, inure; to naturalize

def: v. n. To succeed, to answer, to suit, to come to pass.

s.m. Bridegroom ( = bana, bannā, q.v.). s.m. The woof (as opp. to tānā, 'warp'); a silken thread used in weaving and sewing, &c.; a kind of hemispherical bucket made of iron plates closely riveted together, used in raising water for irrigation.
s.f. Bride ( = banī).
s.m. The bamboo, Bambusa arundinacea and stricta; a bamboo or rod, about ten feet long, used in measuring land, &c.
more: n. m. S. वंश A bamboo; a rod or bamboo about ten feet long, used to measure fields, excavations, etc. 1. Ādmī hai, Sirāṅche kā bāṅs! Phabtī . Chaff. 2. Mallāhī kī mallāhī dī, bāṅs ke bāṅs khāë . Prov. My fare first paid, then bamboos on me laid. ☞ Passengers by boat are liable to be struck on the head by the long bamboos used as levers for pushing along the boat. bāṅs par ṭāṅg'nā, yā chaṛhā'nā , v. a. To set up on a bamboo or tight rope, hence to expose; disgrace. Saīyāṅ ho gaë sāṅgī, bāṅs pe mo ko ṭāṅgī Āp luṭāwe māl, mujhe ghar mile na āṅgī . Play. Chaubolā . A showman is my spouse, on bamboo tall I'm shown, He takes the pence, while I at home no stitch can own! bāṅs par chaṛh'nā , v. n. 1. To climb up a bamboo. Jab naṭnī bāṅs par chaṛhī, to ghūṅgaṭ kyā? Prov. A danseuse mounted on a bamboo needs no veil. 2. To get a bad name; to become notorious; to be branded with infamy. bāṅs-phoṛ, …

s. m. 1. A bamboo. 2. A measure of about ten feet, used to measure tanks, ditches, and excavations in general. (A cube of one bāṅs is called a chaukā ). بانس پرچڙهنا bāṅs par chaṛhnā, v. n. To be branded with infamy.
subs: bāṅs-par ćaṛhānā, v.t. To stick or raise up on a bamboo; (fig.) to hold up to ridicule, infamy, &c., to disgrace
v.n. To climb up a bamboo; to be stuck up on a bamboo; to be held up to ridicule, infamy, &c., to be branded with infamy
(S. vaṅśa+sphoṭa), s.m. lit. 'Bamboo-splitter'; a caste who engage in bamboo-work, manufacturing baskets, &c. out of split bamboos (syn. basoṛ, ćohāṛ, ḍom)
bāṅsoṅ kūdnā, v.n. To leap or bound for joy, to rejoice.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀁𑀲 (vaṃsa), from Sanskrit वंश (vaṃśa), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *wanćás.

def: s.m. Bamboo (used in comp. for bāṅs, q.v.); line of a pedigree or genealogy (from its resemblance to the succession of joints in a bamboo), lineage, race, family, stock; descendant, offspring
subs: (S. vaṅśa+āvalī), s.f. Line of a family, pedigree, genealogy, genealogical tree (syn. shajra)
(S. vaṅśa+sphoṭaka), s.m. lit. 'Bamboo-splitter'; one who makes baskets, chairs, &c. of split bamboos or reeds ( = basoṛ, q.v.)
(S. vaṅśa+roćanā or loćanā), s.m. An earthy concretion of a milk-white colour found in the hollow of the bamboo and known by the name of bamboo-manna (syn. tabāshīr)
s.f. Bamboo plantation; jungle of bamboos.

def: n. S. वंश 1. Extraction; descent; genealogy; pedigree; stock. 2. Race; line; lineage; house; family.
more: s. m. 1. Race, lineage, family, offspring, descendant. 2. A bamboo.

def: s.m. Bridge of the nose; back-bone, spine (syn. rīṛh); the bamboo tube through which the seed descends in a drilling machine; a drill-plough; — a plant of the bamboo species (a variety of the Dendrocalamus Tulda or pekā-bāṅs) from which a red dye is extracted (syn. piyā-bāṅsā).
s.m. A species of grass which grows in rice fields (it is used as fodder for cattle).
W. n. m. 1. A drill-plough (Brij. najaro ). 2. The bridge of the nose (E. banāsā; Tir. nak-bāṅsā ,); the back-bone ( rīṛh ). 3. A plant from the leaves of which a red dye is extracted ( pīyā bāṅsā ).
more: s. m. The bridge of the nose.

def: s.f. A weed (found in the Doāb), which is very injurious, and most difficult to eradicate.
wety: Borrowed from Braj बाँसुरी (bā̃surī). Displaced بَانسْلِی (bānslī).

def: s.f. See s.v. baṅs. s.m. The bamboo, Bambusa arundinacea and stricta; a bamboo or rod, about ten feet long, used in measuring land, &c.
more: n. f. S. वंशावलि A genealogical table or tree; genealogy. bans param parā ; A naslan bād naslan , Hereditary; in one continuous line. nir-bansī , adj. Childless; heirless ( be-aulādā ). Nir-bansyā hai, iskā mūṅh mat dekho .
subs: bāṅs-par ćaṛhānā, v.t. To stick or raise up on a bamboo; (fig.) to hold up to ridicule, infamy, &c., to disgrace
v.n. To climb up a bamboo; to be stuck up on a bamboo; to be held up to ridicule, infamy, &c., to be branded with infamy

bāṅsoṅ kūdnā, v.n. To leap or bound for joy, to rejoice.

def: adj. of or pertaining to bamboo; made of bamboo.
more: n. f. 1. A reed of which hookah-snakes are made. It is also used by weavers and artificial flower makers. 2. A kind of stone.
1. s. f. Flute, fife, pipe. 2. adj. Made of bamboo.

def: s.f. Flute, pipe, &c. = bāṅsrī, q.v.; — reed of which ḥuqqa snakes are made; reed used by weavers and makers of artificial flowers; — a kind of stone.
s.f. Flute, pipe; a kind of wheat with blackish ears
more: n. f. A flute. See بانسري 1. A fishing-rod, or hook. 2. A sort of wheat.
s. f. 1. ( वंशी ) A flute. بنسي بٿ bansī baṭ, The fig tree under which Kr̤ish̤ṇa was accustomed to play the flute. 2. ( वडिश ) A fishing hook. 3. (in compos. from بنس) Of the race or lineage.
subs: s.m. The tree under which Kr̤ishṇa was accustomed to play the flute.

def: adj. Pertaining to a family, race, &c.; lineal, genealogical; of the race or lineage (of).
s.f. Fishing-hook; fishing-line or tackle.
s.f. Flute, &c. = bāṅsrī, q.v.; — a purse ( = bāṅsnī). s.f. Pipe, fife, flute, flageolet
more: s. f. 1. A flute, &c.: see بانسري bāṅsrī. 2. A purse: see بسني basnī.
subs: v.n. To pipe, to play on the flute.

def: n. m. S. बंसलोचन H. bāṅs bamboo, lochan eye, hence a joint. A white flinty substance found in the joints of Bamboo (A. tabāshīr ).
s. m. The manna or sugar of the bamboo. Arab. طباشير. (It has been found by analysis to contain much siliceous earth).
s.f. A purse ( = basnī, q.v.). s.f. Purse (syn. khuttī, thailī).
s. m. Name of a cast who work on bamboos.
s.f. Flute, &c. = bāṅsrī, baṅsī qq.v. s.f. Pipe, fife, flute, flageolet
subs: v.n. To pipe, to play on the flute.

def: s.f. Pipe, fife, flute, flageolet
more: n. f. S. वंशी A flute; pipe; flageolet. 1. Morī Rādhā ne bansī churāī, Ab maiṅ na jīūṅ, morī māī . Brij. Song. Rādhā my pipe has hidden away, I will not, mother, live a day. 2. Ham tajo Brij ko basibo, Ab to bas bairan tū basrī . Brij. Song. Go, pipe, I'm gone from Brij for aye, Go, reign alone, my foe, good bye. (The captivating melodies from Krishnā's pipe were too much for her feelings). bāṅsrī bajā'nā , v. n. To pipe; play on the flute.
s. f. A flute, fife, pipe.
subs: v.n. To pipe, to play on the flute.
wety: Borrowed from Braj बाँसुरी (bā̃surī). Displaced بَانسْلِی (bānslī).

def: s.f. Bamboo thicket; bamboo plantation ( = baṅswāṛī).
s.f. See s.v. baṅs. s.m. The bamboo, Bambusa arundinacea and stricta; a bamboo or rod, about ten feet long, used in measuring land, &c.
subs: bāṅs-par ćaṛhānā, v.t. To stick or raise up on a bamboo; (fig.) to hold up to ridicule, infamy, &c., to disgrace
v.n. To climb up a bamboo; to be stuck up on a bamboo; to be held up to ridicule, infamy, &c., to be branded with infamy
(S. vaṅśa+sphoṭa), s.m. lit. 'Bamboo-splitter'; a caste who engage in bamboo-work, manufacturing baskets, &c. out of split bamboos (syn. basoṛ, ćohāṛ, ḍom)
bāṅsoṅ kūdnā, v.n. To leap or bound for joy, to rejoice.

def: n. f. A bamboo plantation; a jungle of bamboos.

def: n. f. S. वट् to divide. 1. The act of dividing; division; distribtuion. 2. Allotment; portion; part ( bhāg ); separate part; share; quota. 3. The act (or turn) of dealing the cards. 4. Food given to a cow while milking her. 5. Concern; business; lot or part; interest. bāṅṭ-batāū ; P. nasab-numā ; S. hara , n. m. ( Math .) Denominator. bāṅṭ-patr ; P. taqsīm-nāmā , n. m. Record of division of property. bāṅṭ-phal ; A. khārij-qismat ; S. लब्धि ( Math .) Quotient. bāṅṭ-chūṅṭ,—būṅṭ , n. f. See bāṅṭ (1). an-baṅṭā , adj. Undivided; not partitioned; whole.
s.m. Dividing, &c.; share, portion, &c. = bāṅṭ, q.v.; — tying up the crops into bundles and sheaves.
adj. Divided, apportioned, &c.
subs: adj. = bāṅṭā; — s.m. (in Arith.), Dividend (also baṭā-huʼā, syn. bhājya, and maqsūm).

def: s.m. A large brass water-pot.
n. m. Tying up the crops into sheaves and trusses.
s.m. Allotter, distributer, divider, &c. = baṭait, baṭwaiyā, qq.v
v.t. To cause to be shared; to distribute, share, divide, &c. = baṭānā, baṭwānā, qq.v.
more: v. a. To share, to divide, to participate.

def: s. f. A name of a plant. (Luffa).
adv. Adorned, embellished, elaborately decorated or dressed up.
adv. Adorned, embellished, elaborately decorated or dressed up.
v.t. = bāṅṭnā, 'to divide,' &c.

v.n. To be twisted, &c. = baṭnā, q.v. v.t. To share, &c. = bāṅṭnā, q.v. v.t. To divide, apportion, distribute, portion out, parcel out, divide into shares; to dispense, dispose of, assign; to participate, go shares in
subs: v.t. To divide, portion out (for, or in the name of, -par), &c. = bāṅṭnā, q.v.
v.t. To participate, go shares in, &c.

def: v.t. To divide, apportion, distribute, portion out, parcel out, divide into shares; to dispense, dispose of, assign; to participate, go shares in

def: v. a. To divide; distribute; apportion; dispense; assign. Chamelī chāo meṅ āī, Baḳhtyārā revṛīyāṅ bāṅṭe . Prov. The jasmine fair now loving grown, the favored one dispenses sweets. bāṅṭā-huā, yā baṭā-huā ; S. bhājya ; A. maqsūm . ( Math .) Dividend. bāṅṭū ; S. bhājak ; A. maqsūm-ilah . ( Math .) Divisor.
v.t. To twist, twine.

def: s.m. Divider, apportioner, &c.; (in Arith.), divisor (syn. bhājak, and maqsūm-ilaih).
s.m. One who allots shares, one who distributes; divider, distributer ( = baṭwaiyā, q.v.).
v.t. = baṭwānā, baṭānā, qq.v. v.t. To cause to be divided, &c.; to distribute.
more: v. a. To distribute, to cause to be shared.

def: s.m. = baṭwārā, q.v. s.m. Partition, share; division or partition of land; allotment of shares; separation of coparceners, detachment of the share of an individual coparcener; — deed or document under which partition of land is made (syn. baṭāʼī-patr)
subs: v.t. To divide, share; to make a partition of land.

def: n. 1. Concern; business; interest. Hamārā terā bāṅṭyā kyā? 2. Cause; occasion; ground; reason. Laṛāī kā kyā bāṅṭye? Wom. an-baṭā āṅk, pūrā āṅk; A. adad-i-sahīh; S. अभिन्न n. m. ( Math .) Integer; whole number.
